reason: Response A shows the controlled literary craft genuine Sonnet tends toward: a sustained metaphor (the weekend as defective product / a trick of perspective), genuinely fresh images ("time were a bird and not a thief"), and a restrained, earned ending rather than a punchline. Response B leans on the more imitable "AI humor" tics—staccato fragments ("Two days. Two."), sarcastic one-word sentences ("Magnificent." "Remarkable efficiency."), and a generic mock-review sign-off—which reads like a model performing Sonnet's wit rather than producing it. The tighter prose discipline and lack of formulaic snark point to A as the authentic Sonnet.
